tuto.step.1: Welcome to <bold>Ipso</bold>! Your goal: fill your pyramid with numbered cards and score as many points as possible. Click on the <bold>3</bold> card in the display to place it in the bottom-left slot of the pyramid.
tuto.step.2: The 3 card is in place! Your goal: form <bold>ascending rows</bold> in your pyramid. The bottom row has 5 cards — if they go from smallest to largest, they score <bold>1 point per card</bold>. All the <bold>same color</bold>? The points are <bold>doubled</bold>!
tuto.step.3: Your turn! Now place the <bold>7</bold> card in the bottom-left slot of the second row.
tuto.step.4: Some cards have <bold>stars</bold> — each star visible on your cards at the end of the game scores <bold>1 extra point</bold>!
tuto.step.5: Keep filling your pyramid turn after turn! Once all your <bold>14 cards</bold> are revealed, you can use your <bold>star card</bold> to draw the top card from the deck and replace a card. Or keep it for <bold>3 bonus points</bold> at the end of the game.
tuto.step.6: At the end of the game, rows whose numbers are not in ascending order are <bold>discarded</bold> and score nothing. The others score 1 or 2 points per card depending on color. Good luck!
header.play-card.you: You must choose a card and place it in your pyramid
header.play-card.player: {player} must choose a card and place it in their pyramid
header.use-star-card.you: You can <use>use your star card</use> or <pass>pass</pass>
header.use-star-card.player: {player} can use their star card to draw the top card from the deck and replace one of their cards
help.number-card.title: Numbered Card
help.number-card.description: Each card has a <bold>number from 1 to 90</bold> and a <bold>color</bold> among five. Some cards also have <bold>stars</bold> (1 or 2) that score bonus points.
help.number-card.colors: There are <bold>5 colors</bold>: pink, yellow, green, cyan, and orange.
help.number-card.pyramid.title: The pyramid
help.number-card.pyramid.description: Each player builds a pyramid of <bold>14 cards</bold> organized in 4 rows. At the start of the game, all cards are hidden. Each turn, you choose a visible card from the display and place it face up in your pyramid, swapping it with a hidden card.
help.number-card.scoring.title: Score
help.number-card.scoring.description: At the end of the game, each row of your pyramid scores points if its cards are in <bold>ascending order</bold> from left to right:
help.number-card.scoring.same-color: An ascending row scores <bold>1 point per card</bold>. If all cards are the <bold>same color</bold>, it scores <bold>2 points per card</bold>. A non-ascending row is discarded and scores nothing.
help.number-card.stars.title: Stars
help.number-card.stars.description: Each star visible on your cards at the end of the game scores <bold>1 extra point</bold>.
help.star-card.title: Star Card
help.star-card.description: Each player has <bold>one star card</bold>, placed at the top of their pyramid at the start of the game.
help.star-card.use.title: Use
help.star-card.use.description: Once all <bold>14 cards in your pyramid are revealed</bold>, you can discard your star card to draw the top card from the deck and replace any card in your pyramid. You can also choose to <bold>pass</bold> and keep your star card.
help.star-card.bonus.title: End-of-game bonus
help.star-card.bonus.description: If your star card is still in your pyramid at the end of the game, it scores <bold>3 extra points</bold>.
oddOrEven.help: The rules remain unchanged. After the regular scoring, add 1 point per card if a row consists entirely of even cards or entirely of odd cards.
